metadata.title: BlockBuilder - Block builder with clean architecture
metadata.description: A library for building block-based editors with clean architecture. Vue 3, React 18+, TypeScript.

hero.subtitle: Block builder with clean architecture
hero.description: Build powerful no-code editors with proper architecture. Vue 3, React 18+, SSR (Nuxt, Next.js). MIT, no restrictions.

cleanArchitecture.title: Clean architecture
cleanArchitecture.description: Built on Clean Architecture principles with clear layers: Core, Infrastructure, and UI.

solid.title: SOLID principles
solid.description: Each class has a single responsibility. Easy to test, extend, and maintain.

events.title: Events

basicExample.title: Basic example
basicExample.description: Create a block component and connect BlockBuilderComponent:

step1.title: 1. Create a block component
step1.spacingNote: Important: external margins are applied automatically to the UI block wrapper above the component. For internal padding, use CSS variables that are set automatically. More about spacing →

multipleTypes.title: Multiple block types
multipleTypes.description: You can define several block types with different components and fields:

editMode.title: Edit/view mode
editMode.description: BlockBuilder supports two modes. Use the isEdit prop to control the mode:

viewMode.title: What happens in view mode?
items: All edit, add and block management controls are hidden
Only custom block markup remains inside the BlockBuilder wrapper
The body element automatically gets/removes the CSS class bb-is-edit-mode

tips.title: 💡 Important points
items: Spacing — BlockBuilder automatically adds spacing fields to the block settings form when spacingOptions.enabled: true is set. External margins (margin) are applied automatically to the UI block wrapper; for internal padding use CSS variables: --spacing-padding-top and --spacing-padding-bottom
Block properties — your component receives a block object with id, type, props, settings fields
Saving — use the onSave callback to save blocks (localStorage for development or API for production)
Events — subscribe to component events to sync with your backend
View mode — use :isEdit="false" to display blocks in view-only mode without editing

coreWithoutComponent.title: Core without BlockBuilderComponent
coreWithoutComponent.description: If you need your own editor UI, use @mushket-co/block-builder/core directly. In Vue, create an instance in onMounted, store it in ref, call methods via await blockBuilder.value?.createBlock(...).
coreWithoutComponent.footer: Methods and types reference — <apiOverview>API overview</apiOverview>, <methods>Methods</methods>. Example — <example>examples/vue3-core-api</example>.

components.title: Components
components.subtitle: Vue 3 BlockBuilder components and their API

blockBuilderComponent.description: The main component for working with BlockBuilder in Vue 3. Ready-made UI: block list, create and edit forms, reorder with buttons, delete.

config.description: BlockBuilder configuration with block types and settings

blockManagementUseCase.description: Use case instance for block management. Created via createBlockManagementUseCase()

initialBlocks.description: Initial blocks to load on initialization

onSave.description: Callback for saving blocks (e.g. sending to server)

isEdit.description: Edit mode. If false, all edit controls are hidden; only custom block markup remains inside the BlockBuilder wrapper.

controlsContainerClass.description: Custom CSS class for the controls container (buttons and stats)

controlsFixedPosition.description: Fixes the control panel (buttons + stats) at the top or bottom of the screen

locale.description: Built-in BlockBuilder UI localization (buttons, control labels, confirm texts). Block field labels are set by the application.

uiStrings.description: Point overrides for strings on top of the locale preset.

theme.description: UI theme preset: default (when omitted) or dark. Override tokens via themeVars on .bb-app.

warnOnPageLeave.description: Warning when leaving the page with unsaved blocks

notes.title: Create and edit block forms
notes.formValidation: Rules from fields[].rules are validated automatically in the BlockBuilder modal — no separate validator wiring needed. If the user clicks Save with errors, messages appear next to fields and update on every change.
notes.blockAnchor: A block-anchor field stores an anchor (#block-id or URL). Implement scroll and click navigation in your block components. Fields file and files use a dedicated upload UI in the editor.

events.blockAdded: Block created or duplicated — full block object passed to callback
events.blockUpdated: Block updated — current object after props, settings, etc. change
events.blockDeleted: Block deleted — id only (string), not the object

blockComponents.title: Your block components
blockComponents.description: Each block type uses your Vue component for rendering. The component receives a block object with full block information.
importantItems: The component must accept a block prop
Spacing is applied automatically: margin via inline styles, padding via CSS variables (--spacing-padding-top, --spacing-padding-bottom, etc.)
All custom props are available via block.props
The component must be reactive — prop changes automatically update the block

events.title: Events
events.subtitle: BlockBuilderComponent events for tracking block changes
events.reorderNote: Reordering: track via onSave or blockManagementUseCase

blockAdded.description: Fires after creating a new block or duplicating an existing one

blockUpdated.description: Fires when props, settings or other block properties change

blockDeleted.description: Fires after the user deletes a block

tips.title: 💡 Tips
items: Use debounce for autosave to avoid overloading the server with requests
For optimization, you can batch-save several blocks at once
Handle save errors and show notifications to the user
Events fire synchronously with user actions — use them for validation

gettingStarted.title: Quick start with React
gettingStarted.subtitle: Get started with BlockBuilder in React — ready-made UI and full integration with React components (React 18+ / 19)

install.description: Install BlockBuilder and include editor styles:
install.typescriptNote: TypeScript types are included in the package; no separate @types install needed.

blockBuilderComponent.title: BlockBuilderComponent
blockBuilderComponent.description: Main React component that provides the ready-made block management UI

yourComponents.title: Your components
yourComponents.description: React components for rendering specific block types; register them via blockManagementUseCase.getComponentRegistry()

config.title: Config
config.description: Configuration with available block types, their fields and components

basicExample.title: Basic example
basicExample.description: Create a block component and wire up BlockBuilderComponent:

step1.title: 1. Create a block component
step1.spacingNote: Important: outer spacing (margin) is applied automatically on the block wrapper above your component. For inner spacing (padding), use CSS variables set automatically. More about spacing →

multipleTypes.title: Multiple block types
multipleTypes.description: You can define several block types with different components and fields:

editMode.title: Edit / view mode
editMode.description: BlockBuilder supports two modes. Use the isEdit prop to control the mode:

viewMode.title: What happens in view mode?
items: All edit, add and block management controls are hidden
Only your block markup remains inside the BlockBuilder wrapper
CSS class bb-is-edit-mode is added/removed on body automatically

tips.title: 💡 Important notes
items: Component registration — create createBlockManagementUseCase() once (e.g. in useMemo) and register block React components via getComponentRegistry().register(type, Component)

Spacing — BlockBuilder adds spacing fields to the block settings form when spacingOptions.enabled: true. Outer spacing (margin) is applied on the block wrapper; for inner spacing (padding) use CSS variables: --spacing-padding-top and --spacing-padding-bottom
Block props — your component receives a block prop with id, type, props, settings
Saving — use the onSave callback to persist blocks (localStorage for development or API in production)
Callbacks — onBlockAdded / onBlockUpdated / onBlockDeleted for backend sync
View mode — use isEdit={false} to display blocks without editing controls

coreWithoutComponent.title: Core without BlockBuilderComponent
coreWithoutComponent.description: For a custom editor UI, use @mushket-co/block-builder/core directly. In React, create an instance in useEffect, store it in useRef or state, call methods via await blockBuilder.createBlock(...).
coreWithoutComponent.footer: Methods and types reference — <apiOverview>API overview</apiOverview>, <methods>Methods</methods>. Example — <example>examples/react18</example>.

next.description: For server-side rendering with Next.js App Router, see <nextLink>Next.js (SSR)</nextLink>. Live example — <nextExample>examples/next</nextExample> in the block-builder repo (not in demo-bb).

components.title: Components
components.subtitle: React BlockBuilder components and their API

blockBuilderComponent.description: Main component for BlockBuilder in React. Ready-made UI: block list, create/edit forms, reorder with buttons, delete.

config.description: BlockBuilder configuration with block types and settings

blockManagementUseCase.description: Use case instance for block management. Created via createBlockManagementUseCase()

onSave.description: Callback to save blocks (e.g. send to server)

isEdit.description: Edit mode. When false, all edit controls are hidden; only your block markup remains inside the BlockBuilder wrapper.

locale.description: Built-in BlockBuilder UI localization (buttons, control labels, confirm texts). Block field labels are set by the application.

uiStrings.description: Override individual UI strings on top of the locale preset.

theme.description: UI theme preset: default (when omitted) or dark. Override tokens via themeVars on .bb-app.

warnOnPageLeave.description: Warning when leaving the page with unsaved blocks

controlsContainerClass.description: Custom CSS class for the controls container (buttons and stats)

controlsFixedPosition.description: Fixes the control panel (buttons + stats) to the top or bottom of the screen

notes.title: Create and edit block forms
notes.formValidation: Rules from fields[].rules are validated automatically in the BlockBuilder modal — no separate validator wiring needed. If the user clicks Save with errors, messages appear next to fields and update on every change.
notes.blockAnchor: A block-anchor field stores an anchor (#block-id or URL). Implement scroll and click navigation in your block components. Fields file and files use a dedicated upload UI in the editor.

callbacks.onBlockAdded: Block created or duplicated — full block object in the callback
callbacks.onBlockUpdated: Block updated — current object after props, settings, etc. change
callbacks.onBlockDeleted: Block deleted — id only (string), not the object

blockComponents.title: Your block components
blockComponents.description: Each block type uses your React component for rendering. The component receives a block prop with full block information.
blockComponents.footer: Full list of block type props — in <formFields>Form fields</formFields>. Spacing (margin/padding) — <spacing>spacing</spacing>.
importantItems: The component must accept a block prop
Spacing is applied automatically: margin via inline styles, padding via CSS variables (--spacing-padding-top, --spacing-padding-bottom, etc.)
All custom props are available via block.props
The component must be registered in getComponentRegistry() before the editor renders

events.title: Callbacks
events.subtitle: BlockBuilderComponent callbacks for tracking block changes
events.reorderNote: Block reordering — via onSave or blockManagementUseCase

onBlockAdded.description: Fires after creating a new block or duplicating an existing one

onBlockUpdated.description: Fires when props, settings or other block fields change
onBlockUpdated.payload: block: IBlock — current object after change

onBlockDeleted.description: Fires after the user deletes a block

onSaveVsCallbacks.description: onBlockAdded / onBlockUpdated / onBlockDeleted — point reactions to user actions. onSave receives the entire blocks array on explicit save (Save button) and can return false to keep unsaved state.

items: For autosave, use debounce in onBlockUpdated
On delete, save only the id — the block object is no longer available
Duplicating a block also triggers onBlockAdded
Callbacks fire synchronously with user actions in the UI

code.blockCreatedComment: block — full created or duplicated block object
code.blockIdDeletedComment: blockId — deleted block id (string), not the object

nuxt.title: Nuxt (SSR)
nuxt.subtitle: Integrating @mushket-co/block-builder/vue in Nuxt 3/4 with server-side rendering of block content
nuxt.demoNote: Live SSR demo for Nuxt is not in interactive demo-bb (Vue 3 and React 18 there). Run ready-made projects from the package repo: examples/nuxt3 and examples/nuxt4 in block-builder/examples.
nuxt.installNote: Include styles in a client-only plugin — CSS should not end up in the SSR bundle unnecessarily.
nuxt.nuxtConfigNote: The /vue entry ships raw .ts and .vue — without transpile the build will fail.

composable.description: You create this file yourself — the package has no composable. It assembles dependencies for BlockBuilderComponent: use case, block types from block-config, edit mode and API save.

items.availableBlockTypes: availableBlockTypes — type config for palette and forms

composable.serializeNote: serializeBlocksForStorage — project utility: strips render.component and base64 before JSON. Example — examples/nuxt3/utils/serializeBlocks.ts. For api-select and custom fields the example also wires ApiSelectUseCase and CustomFieldRendererRegistry.

ssrPage.description: On the server, load blocks; the composable from the previous step passes props to the component.

apiSave.description: On the server, load blocks from DB/file; when serving to the client, enrich data for api-select blocks. Before saving, serialize without render.component and SSR cache.

ssrUtils.autoNote: prepareBlocksForDisplay and seedRepositoryFromBlocks are called inside the component automatically.

liveExamples.title: Live examples in the repository
liveExamples.runNote: From monorepo root: npm run example:nuxt3 or npm run example:nuxt4
liveExamples.vueApi: Vue component API — in the <vueLink>Vue 3</vueLink> section.

serverClient.description: Server Component loads and enriches blocks → Client Component renders the editor with initialBlocks.

ssrUtils.enrichNote: enrichBlocksForSsr in the example is a project function for preloading api-select data (newsList, etc.) on the server.

liveExample.title: Live example in the repository
liveExample.reactApi: Basic React API — in the <reactLink>React</reactLink> section.

changelog.title: Changelog
changelog.subtitle: Brief release history. Full list — in the repository <changelog>CHANGELOG.md</changelog>.

highlights: UI localization: locale ru/en, uiStrings, no external i18n libraries
Theming via CSS variables: theme="dark", themeVars on .bb-app
ValidationErrorHandler: expand nested repeater at arbitrary depth
React UI component tests on both major versions
Programmatic API and separate Vue/React UI entries
Code splitting for heavy form elements
npm run size — initial vs async chunks report

classes.title: API overview
classes.subtitle: The headless layer @mushket-co/block-builder/core is open domain logic without a ready-made editor. Vue/React UI on top is optional, not mandatory.

why.title: What is core
why.p1: BlockBuilder is a facade: block CRUD, field schema, validation, custom form renderers, import/export. No ready-made editor panel — that comes from @mushket-co/block-builder/vue and /react, using the same rules and types.
why.p2: Core does not force you to abandon UI. It is a fallback clean layer: build your own editor or work with data only; if a ready-made one suits you — use the Vue/React component.

when.title: When to use what
when.intro: The choice is yours. Below are typical scenarios, not dogma.
when.componentDesc: Ready-made editor out of the box — quick start if its UX and UI work for you.
when.componentSsrSuffix: (server render blocks + hydrate editor in admin)
when.coreDesc: When the ready-made UI does not fit or you do not need an editor at all — logic and data only.
when.coreAdminUi: Custom admin UI: site design system, strict layouts — built-in editor does not fit
when.coreCustomEditor: Custom editor on core (forms, lists, block order — as you decide)
when.coreDataOnly: Data only: scripts, tests, API without mounting UI (Node and browser)

code.blockConfigsComment: required: block type schema and fields

example.title: Minimal example
example.intro: Block type config defines form fields; methods create and update blocks in the repository (in-memory by default).

methods.title: Methods
methods.subtitle: API methods for working with blocks

crud.title: CRUD operations
crud.createBlock: Creates a new block with the specified parameters
crud.getAllBlocks: Returns a list of all created blocks
crud.updateBlock: Updates an existing block by identifier

editMode.title: Edit/view mode
editMode.setIsEdit: Toggles edit mode. When false, all edit controls are hidden; only custom block markup remains
editMode.getIsEdit: Returns the current edit mode (true — edit mode, false — view mode)
editMode.note: In view mode (isEdit: false) all edit, add, and block management controls are automatically hidden. Only custom block markup remains inside the BlockBuilder wrapper. The body element automatically gets the CSS class bb-is-edit-mode for style customization.

customRenderers.title: Custom renderers
customRenderers.registerCustomFieldRenderers: Bulk registration of multiple custom field renderers.
customRenderers.getCustomFieldRenderer: Gets a custom renderer by ID.
customRenderers.hasCustomFieldRenderer: Checks if a custom renderer exists.

blockConfigs.description: Block type schema. Key is type, value is IBlockTypeConfig (form fields, render, spacing).
blockConfigs.seeAlso: See also: <classesExampleLink>core example</classesExampleLink> · <formFieldsLink>form fields</formFieldsLink> · <vueComponentsLink>Vue / React config</vueComponentsLink>

autoInit.description: Load initialBlocks into the repository when the instance is created.

themingLocalization.title: UI theming and localization
themingLocalization.subtitle: BlockBuilder (Vue/React) supports built-in string localization and appearance overrides via CSS custom properties on .bb-app.

locale.title: UI localization
locale.p1: The package localizes only its UI (buttons, control labels, validation messages). Field labels in block-config are set by the application.
locale.hooksNote: React: useUiStrings() inside UI. Vue: useUiStrings() via provide/inject.

themePreset.title: theme presets
themePreset.p1: The theme prop is TUiTheme: default (default value, light palette) or dark. CSS variables are written to the root .bb-app element.
themePreset.coreNote: In headless core without UI rendering, theme and locale have no behavioral effect. In BlockBuilderComponent they are passed together with themeVars and uiStrings.

themeVars.title: themeVars customization
themeVars.description: Any --bb-* tokens can be overridden with a themeVars object.

teleportedUi.title: Teleported UI (dropdown)
teleportedUi.p1: CustomDropdown panels mount to body and lose CSS variable inheritance. The package automatically copies theme vars from the nearest .bb-app when opening a dropdown (Vue/React CustomDropdown).
teleportedUi.p2: For your own teleported UI you can use the internal helper readBbThemeVarsFromClosestApp from shared/theme/bbThemeContext (package pattern).

seeAlso.title: See also
seeAlso.properties: Properties — theme, locale, and other parameters

utilities.title: Utilities
utilities.subtitle: Helper functions exported from the package and available for use in your application

scrollLock.title: Scroll lock
scrollLock.description: Document scroll lock management. Exported from @mushket-co/block-builder/core.
scrollLock.lockBodyScroll: lockBodyScroll/unlockBodyScroll — default implementations: add/remove CSS class, account for scrollbar width.
scrollLock.tip1: Use the same strategy for modals, dropdowns, overlays — do not mix approaches.
scrollLock.tip2: With a custom implementation, account for scrollbar width compensation (padding-right).
scrollLock.importantNote: If your framework already manages scroll lock (e.g. a third-party modal library), override handlers via setScrollLockHandlers and delegate to the library.

blockHierarchy.title: Block helper functions
blockHierarchy.buildBlockHierarchy: buildBlockHierarchy — converts a flat list into a tree by parent/children fields.
blockHierarchy.cloneBlock: cloneBlock — deep-clones a block object (no references to the original).
blockHierarchy.getAllChildren: getAllChildren — returns all descendants (at any nesting level).
blockHierarchy.normalizeNote: Normalize input data: ensure unique id values and consistent parent⇄children links before calling helper functions.

spacing.title: Spacing utilities
spacing.description: Exported from @mushket-co/block-builder/core: DEFAULT_BREAKPOINTS, generateSpacingCSS, generateSpacingCSSVariables, getSpacingValue, mergeSpacing, setSpacingValue, validateSpacing.
spacing.generateSpacingCSSVariables: generateSpacingCSSVariables — convenient for passing padding via CSS variables to a component.
spacing.generateSpacingCSS: generateSpacingCSS — suitable for SSR/style injection on the page (e.g. a "<"style">" tag).
spacing.mergeSpacing: mergeSpacing — merges configurations cleanly (without overwriting missing keys).
spacing.validateSpacing: validateSpacing — use in backend or CI validation of configurations.
spacing.uiNote: In the UI version BlockBuilder applies spacing automatically. These helpers are useful for manual integration (/core) or generating styles outside UI components.

filterBlocks.title: Block visibility
filterBlocks.description: filterBlocksForDisplay(blocks, isEdit) — export from core. In view mode hidden blocks (visible: false) are excluded from the DOM; in edit mode they remain with the bb-opacity-hidden class.

unsavedChanges.title: Unsaved changes
unsavedChanges.description: For custom UI without ready-made BlockBuilderComponent — utilities and hooks from core / vue / react. The warnOnPageLeave option in components enables native browser warning.

reactiveValidation.title: Reactive form validation
reactiveValidation.description: For custom form UI (without ready-made BlockBuilderComponent) — tracker and helper from @mushket-co/block-builder/core. In BlockBuilderComponent reactive validation is enabled automatically: after a failed submit, errors update on every field change.
reactiveValidation.touch: touch() — call after a failed submit to enable revalidation on every change.
reactiveValidation.revalidateIfTouched: revalidateIfTouched — returns current errors or null if the form is not yet "touched".
reactiveValidation.applyFormErrors: applyFormErrors — atomically replaces the errors object (convenient for Vue reactive / React state).

universalValidator.title: UniversalValidator
universalValidator.message: The message field is optional; default texts are available.
universalValidator.customNote: For complex cases use the custom rule with your own validation function. Return false on failure and set message for a readable error.

types.title: Types
types.subtitle: TypeScript types and interfaces for working with BlockBuilder

iblockBuilderOptions.code:
interface IBlockBuilderOptions {
  blockConfigs: Record<string, any>
  repository?: any
  componentRegistry?: any
  httpClient?: any
  customFieldRendererRegistry?: any
  theme?: TUiTheme
  locale?: 'ru' | 'en'
  autoInit?: boolean
  initialBlocks?: IBlockDto[]
}

iblock.code:
interface IBlock {
  id: TBlockId
  type: string
  settings: Record<string, any>
  props: Record<string, any>
  style?: Record<string, string | number>
  render?: any // Internal TRenderRef type (not exported)
  visible?: boolean
  formConfig?: IFormGenerationConfig
  metadata?: IBlockMetadata
  children?: IBlock[]
  parent?: TBlockId
}

iblockDto.description: DTO for transferring block data (no business logic)
iblockDto.code:
interface IBlockDto {
  id: string
  type: string
  settings: Record<string, any>
  props: Record<string, any>
  style?: Record<string, string | number>
  render?: any // Internal TRenderRef type (not exported)
  visible?: boolean
  formConfig?: IFormGenerationConfig
  metadata?: IBlockMetadata
  children?: string[] // Child block IDs
  parent?: string
  order?: number
}

icreateBlockDto.description: DTO for creating a block (without ID and metadata)
icreateBlockDto.code:
interface ICreateBlockDto {
  type: string
  settings: Record<string, any>
  props: Record<string, any>
  style?: Record<string, string | number>
  render?: any // Internal TRenderRef type (not exported)
  visible?: boolean
  formConfig?: IFormGenerationConfig
  parent?: string
  order?: number
  metadata?: IBlockMetadata
}

iupdateBlockDto.code:
interface IUpdateBlockDto {
  settings?: Partial<Record<string, any>>
  props?: Partial<Record<string, any>>
  style?: Partial<Record<string, string | number>>
  order?: number
  render?: any // Internal TRenderRef type (not exported)
  visible?: boolean
  formConfig?: IFormGenerationConfig
}

iformFieldConfig.code:
interface IFormFieldConfig {
  field: string
  label: string
  type: TFieldType
  placeholder?: string
  defaultValue?: any
  options?: { value: string; label: string }[]
  rules?: IValidationRule[]
  spacingConfig?: any // Internal type (not exported)
  repeaterConfig?: any // Internal type (not exported)
  apiSelectConfig?: IApiSelectConfig
  customFieldConfig?: any // Internal type (not exported)
  fileUploadConfig?: any // Internal IFileUploadConfig type (not exported)
  blockAnchorConfig?: IBlockAnchorConfig // type: 'block-anchor'
  matrixTableConfig?: IMatrixTableFieldConfig // type: 'matrix-table'
  optionsFrom?: IOptionsFromConfig // select — dynamic options
  fileImportConfig?: IFileImportConfig // type: 'file-import'
  persist?: boolean // false — do not save to block.props
  multiple?: boolean // image, file; select with multiple: true
  dependsOn?: IDependsOnConfig // Conditional field visibility
}

irepeaterFieldConfig.description: Configuration for a repeater field. The countLabelVariants property localizes the item counter. The maxNestingDepth property limits maximum repeater nesting depth.
irepeaterFieldConfig.note: By default the UI shows only the number. If countLabelVariants is set, it shows "number + word". The maxNestingDepth property limits maximum repeater nesting depth (default 2).

irepeaterItemFieldConfig.description: Configuration of a single field in the repeaterConfig.fields array. Same shape as IFormFieldConfig — supports options, apiSelectConfig, matrixTableConfig, dependsOn, persist, and all other form field properties.
irepeaterItemFieldConfig.note: Nested repeater: type: 'repeater' + repeaterConfig. Other field types (select, image, custom, etc.) are configured the same way as in the block's root fields.

iformGenerationConfig.code:
interface IFormGenerationConfig {
  title: string
  description?: string
  fields: IFormFieldConfig[]
  submitButtonText?: string
  cancelButtonText?: string
}

ivalidationRule.code:
interface IValidationRule {
  type: TValidationRuleType
  field: string
  value?: any
  message?: string
  validator?: (value: any) => boolean
}

type TValidationRuleType = 
  | 'required' 
  | 'email' 
  | 'url' 
  | 'min' 
  | 'max' 
  | 'minLength' 
  | 'maxLength' 
  | 'pattern' 
  | 'custom'

iapiSelectItem.description: API list item for api-select field

ifieldValidationConfig.code:
interface IFieldValidationConfig {
  field: string
  label: string
  type: TFieldType
  placeholder?: string
  options?: Array<{ value: any; label: string }>
  rules: IValidationRule[]
  defaultValue?: any
}

iapiSelectConfig.description: Configuration for api-select field. debounceMs defaults to 0 (request immediately on input).
iapiSelectConfig.code:
interface IApiSelectConfig {
  url: string
  method?: 'GET' | 'POST'
  headers?: Record<string, string>
  searchParam?: string
  pageParam?: string
  limitParam?: string
  limit?: number
  debounceMs?: number  // default: 0
  multiple?: boolean
  responseMapper?: (response: any) => IApiSelectResponse
  dataPath?: string
  idField?: string
  nameField?: string
  minSearchLength?: number
  placeholder?: string
  noResultsText?: string
  loadingText?: string
  errorText?: string
}

iblockAnchorConfig.description: Configuration for block-anchor field. The form saves #block-id or URL in props; anchor scroll and click handling are in user block components.

idependsOnConfig.description: Conditional field visibility configuration based on another field value

operators.equals: equals (default) — field is visible when value equals value
operators.notEquals: notEquals — field is visible when value does not equal value
operators.in: in — field is visible when value is in array value
operators.notIn: notIn — field is visible when value is not in array value

iblockFormHooks.description: Create/edit block modal lifecycle hooks. Set on the block type config via formHooks in IBlockTypeConfig / IBlockType.

iblockTypeConfig.description: Configuration of a single block type. In Vue/React — an item in availableBlockTypes[] (exported there as IBlockType). In core blockConfigs — the same fields, but the Record key is the type; title is often used instead of label.

customRenderers.title: Custom renderers
customRenderers.subtitle: Creating custom field renderers to extend form functionality

whatIs.title: What is a custom renderer?
whatIs.p1: A custom renderer lets you create your own UI component for editing a field in a block form. This is especially useful when standard field types are not enough for your needs.

whenItems.libraries: Integration with third-party libraries (e.g. Chart.js, CodeMirror)
whenItems.complexSelect: Complex selection components (multi-level menus, tree select)

interface.title: ICustomFieldRenderer interface
interface.p1: A custom renderer must implement the ICustomFieldRenderer interface:
interface.code:
interface ICustomFieldRenderer {
  readonly id: string
  readonly name: string
  render(
    container: HTMLElement,
    context: ICustomFieldContext
  ): ICustomFieldRenderResult | Promise<ICustomFieldRenderResult>
}

interface ICustomFieldContext {
  fieldName: string
  label: string
  value: unknown
  required: boolean
  options?: Record<string, unknown>  // from customFieldConfig.options
  error?: string                      // validation error text
  formScope?: ICustomFieldFormScope    // formData, setField, repeater
  onChange: (value: unknown) => void
  onError?: (error: string | null) => void
}

interface ICustomFieldRenderResult {
  element: HTMLElement | string
  getValue?: () => unknown
  setValue?: (value: unknown) => void
  setError?: (error: string | null) => void  // without recreating DOM
  validate?: () => string | null
  destroy?: () => void
}

step1.title: Step 1: Create renderer class
step1.p1: Create a class implementing the ICustomFieldRenderer interface:
step1.important: Important for external libraries: store the editor instance locally in render(), not as a class property — critical for repeater. ControlManager guarantees DOM when render() is called: initialize synchronously, without setTimeout. More — custom renderers →

step2.title: Step 2: Register renderer
step2.p1: After creating a BlockBuilder instance, register your renderer:
step2.important: Important: register renderers BEFORE UI initialization (before autoInit: true or creating components). With autoInit: false — before the first form render. More — registration →

step3.title: Step 3: Use in block configuration

datePicker.title: Full example: Date Picker
datePicker.p1: Example of creating a custom renderer for date selection:

async.title: Async initialization
async.p1: If your renderer requires async initialization (e.g. loading libraries), the render method can return a Promise:

api.title: Renderer API
api.registerCustomFieldRenderer: Register a single custom field renderer. Accepts an object implementing ICustomFieldRenderer.
api.registerCustomFieldRenderers: Bulk register multiple renderers. Accepts an array of renderers.
api.getCustomFieldRenderer: Get renderer by ID. Returns ICustomFieldRenderer | null.
api.hasCustomFieldRenderer: Check if renderer exists by ID. Returns boolean.
api.unregisterCustomFieldRenderer: Remove renderer by ID. Returns boolean (whether removal succeeded).
api.getAllCustomFieldRenderers: Get all registered renderers. Returns Map"<"string, ICustomFieldRenderer">".

bestPractices.title: Recommendations and best practices
bestPractices.destroy: Always clean up resources in destroy (remove event listeners, timers, etc.)
bestPractices.onChange: Use onChange to notify about value changes
bestPractices.validate: The validate method should return null when validation succeeds
bestPractices.asyncRender: For complex components use async render with Promise
bestPractices.options: Use options to pass additional configuration parameters

fieldTypes.title: Field types
fieldTypes.subtitle: Supported field types for block editing and creation forms

whatIs.title: What are field types?
whatIs.p1: Field types are UI elements used in block editing and creation forms. When configuring a block type you specify a fields array, and BlockBuilder automatically generates a form with the corresponding controls.

usageExample.title: Usage example

text.description: Single-line text field for short text (headings, titles)
text.example:
{
  field: 'title',
  label: 'Title',
  type: 'text',
  placeholder: 'Enter title...',
  defaultValue: ''
}

textarea.description: Multi-line text field for long text (descriptions, content)
textarea.example:
{
  field: 'content',
  label: 'Content',
  type: 'textarea',
  placeholder: 'Enter text...',
  defaultValue: ''
}

number.description: Number field for numeric input (sizes, counts, values)

select.description: Dropdown for selecting one or multiple values from options. Multiple selection: multiple: true.

radio.description: Radio buttons for selecting one value from a limited set

color.description: Color selection via color picker (HEX format)

image.description: Image upload with base64 and server upload support, automatic preview and validation

file.description: File upload with separate UI (name list, no preview). multiple and maxCount.

blockAnchor.description: Anchor #block-id or URL in form. Scroll, preventDefault and link behavior — in user block component.

field.description: Field name used in block props. Must be unique within the block configuration.

label.description: Display name of the field in the form. Used for the user interface.

defaultValue.description: Default value used when creating a new block.

placeholder.description: Hint displayed in an empty field (for text and textarea types).

options.description: Array of options in format [value, label, disabled?] — e.g. [{ value: '...', label: '...', disabled?: boolean }].

items.select: select: in props — array of value; defaultValue: [] or array of initial values

rules.description: Array of validation rules. Supported types:

formFields.title: Form fields
formFields.subtitle: Field types and custom renderers for block editing and creation forms

overview.title: Overview
overview.description: In block type configuration you define a fields array — BlockBuilder builds the create/edit form and saves values in props.

validation.title: UI validation
validation.description: Errors appear in the modal footer and update when fields change after a failed save. For custom forms without BlockBuilderComponent — use ReactiveFormValidationTracker in the Utilities section. For custom renderers, setError is called only when the error text changes.

usageExample.title: Usage example

fieldTypes.title: Standard field types
fieldTypes.description: Built-in controls for fields. Parameter details — in the field-params section below.

text.example:
{
  field: 'title',
  label: 'Title',
  type: 'text',
  placeholder: 'Enter title...',
  defaultValue: ''
}

textarea.example:
{
  field: 'content',
  label: 'Content',
  type: 'textarea',
  placeholder: 'Enter text...',
  defaultValue: ''
}

select.description: Selection from a fixed options list

radio.description: One option from options, all options visible at once

image.description: Block image with preview and server upload

file.description: Documents and archives — file list, no preview

fileImport.description: Import button: file data merges into other form fields

blockAnchor.description: Link to page block (#id) or external URL

matrixTable.description: Table with headers, HTML and images in cells

custom.description: Custom UI — your renderer by rendererId

fieldParams.title: Field parameters
fieldParams.intro: Each object in the fields array describes one form field. On block save, values go to block.props under the field key (except fields with persist: false).

field.description: Property name in block props. Use it to read values in the render component (props.title, props.cards). Must be unique within the block type.

label.description: Field label in the edit form. Does not affect saved data.

type.description: Which control to show and how to store the value. Allowed parameters depend on type: select needs options, image needs fileUploadConfig, etc. Type list — in field-types section.

defaultValue.description: Value right after creating a new block. Without it — empty string, 0, false or [] depending on type. For repeater — array of items; for select with multiple — array of value.

placeholder.description: Gray hint in an empty input field. Works for text, textarea, email, url.

options.description: Static list of options. value is written to props, label is shown to the user. disabled: true — option is displayed but not selectable.

multiple.description: Multiple values instead of one: in select — array of value, in image/file — multiple uploads. For api-select, multiple mode is set in apiSelectConfig.multiple, not here.

optionsFrom.description: Options are built from another field value — for dependent lists (country → city) without manually duplicating options. source — source field name, when — condition, map — transform to options array. Static options remains fallback.

rules.description: Checks before save. Errors appear in the modal footer. Each rule has optional message — otherwise default text is used.

dependsOn.description: Field is visible only when another field matches a condition. Hidden fields are not validated and do not block save. More — <anchor>depends-on</anchor>.

persist.description: false — field participates in the form but does not go to block.props on save. For service UI: import button, form settings toggles. file-import has persist off by default. Works inside repeater too.

typeConfigs.title: Type-specific configs
typeConfigs.intro: Additional objects — only when standard parameters are not enough:

spacingConfig.description: Block spacing (padding/margin) by breakpoint. spacingOptions at block type config level adds the field automatically; spacingConfig — for manual type: "spacing". See spacing.

repeaterConfig.description: Structure of same-type item array: nested fields, min/max limits, button labels. See repeater.

fileUploadConfig.description: POST file to your server (uploadUrl, responseMapper) instead of storing base64 in props. For image and file.

fileImportConfig.description: File upload as action: API response merges into other form fields via merge (append/replace, dedupe). The field itself is not saved in props.

blockAnchorConfig.description: Select anchor #block-id or custom URL in form. Scroll and click — in your block component. See block-anchor.

matrixTableConfig.description: Table editor settings (tab labels, image size limit in cells). See <anchor>matrix-table</anchor>.

apiSelectConfig.description: API URL, search/pagination parameters, response mapping. See <anchor>api-select</anchor>.

customFieldConfig.description: rendererId — registered renderer id; options — arbitrary settings available in context.options. See custom-renderers.

select.title: Field — list (select)
select.code:
{
  field: 'topics',
  label: 'Topics',
  type: 'select',
  multiple: true,
  options: [
    { value: 'dev', label: 'Development' },
    { value: 'design', label: 'Design' },
    { value: 'marketing', label: 'Marketing' },
    { value: 'analytics', label: 'Analytics' }
  ],
  defaultValue: ['dev', 'design'],
  rules: [{ type: 'required', message: 'Select at least one topic' }]
}

items.multiple: With multiple: true — array of value; defaultValue is also an array (or [])

spacing.title: Field — spacing
spacing.description: Block spacing by breakpoint. Easier to connect via spacingOptions in block type config than a spacing field in fields.
spacing.note: Explicit type: "spacing" field in fields is ignored when spacingOptions is set. Spacing in UI: margin on block wrapper, padding — CSS variables --spacing-padding-* in your component.

repeater.title: Field — repeater
repeater.description: Array of same-type items — cards, slides, list rows. Nested repeaters supported (maxNestingDepth, default 2).
repeater.notes: repeaterConfig.fields — any types except spacing. min without explicit value: 1 when required, else 0. countLabelVariants — counter pluralization ("1 item", "2 items").

image.title: Field — image
image.description: Upload with preview. In props — URL string or object src, width, height, size after server upload. Base64 for development only; in production — fileUploadConfig.uploadUrl.
image.importantNote: Important: with uploadUrl, the server response for image must include src with a URL. For a different API format — responseMapper in fileUploadConfig. file
image.urlHelperCode:
// URL from string or object.src
const imageUrl = typeof props.image === 'string'
  ? props.image
  : props.image?.src ?? ''

file.title: Field — file
file.description: Upload documents and arbitrary files. Name list in form, no preview like image. In props — URL string or URL array with multiple: true.

blockAnchor.title: Field — anchor (block-anchor)
blockAnchor.description: Select page block (#block-id) or custom URL. Scroll and link behavior — in your block component; the form only saves the value.
blockAnchor.scrollExampleIntro: Target block must have data-block-id (BlockBuilder sets it on render):
blockAnchor.scrollExampleCode: {{ text }}

matrixTable.title: Field — table (matrix-table)
matrixTable.description: Table editor: columns and rows, cell types (default, HTML, image). In props — { tableHead, tableBody }.

apiSelect.title: Field — API selection (api-select)
apiSelect.description: Search and pagination via external API. "Load more" — when hasMore: true in response.
apiSelect.code:
{
  field: 'categoryId',
  label: 'Category',
  type: 'api-select',
  apiSelectConfig: {
    url: '/api/categories',
    searchParam: 'search',
    pageParam: 'page',
    limitParam: 'limit',
    limit: 20,
    idField: 'id',
    nameField: 'name',
    minSearchLength: 2,
    responseMapper: (response) => ({
      data: response.items ?? response.data ?? [],
      total: response.total,
      page: response.page,
      hasMore: response.hasMore
    })
  },
  defaultValue: null
}

dependsOn.title: Field — conditional visibility (dependsOn)
dependsOn.description: Field is shown when another field matches a condition. Hidden fields are not validated.
dependsOn.repeaterDescription: dependsOn.field refers to a field in the same repeater item.

customRenderers.title: Custom field renderers
customRenderers.description: Field type: "custom" — when standard types from field-types are not enough: custom widget, library integration, complex interactivity. This is not a separate block type or canvas block component replacement — only field UI in create/edit modal.

whenItems.rating: Rating, color picker, coordinate map, code editor
whenItems.apiData: Data from your API inside one field (not to be confused with <anchor>api-select</anchor>)
whenItems.checkFirst: First check select, repeater, matrix-table — often enough without custom

flowSteps: Register in registry before first form open
customRenderers.interfaceCode:
interface ICustomFieldRenderer {
  readonly id: string
  readonly name: string
  render(
    container: HTMLElement,
    context: ICustomFieldContext
  ): ICustomFieldRenderResult | Promise<ICustomFieldRenderResult>
}

interface ICustomFieldContext {
  fieldName: string
  label: string
  value: unknown
  required: boolean
  options?: Record<string, unknown>  // from customFieldConfig.options
  error?: string                      // validation error text
  formScope?: ICustomFieldFormScope    // formData, setField, repeater
  onChange: (value: unknown) => void
  onError?: (error: string | null) => void
}

interface ICustomFieldRenderResult {
  element: HTMLElement | string
  getValue?: () => unknown
  setValue?: (value: unknown) => void
  setError?: (error: string | null) => void  // without recreating DOM
  validate?: () => string | null
  destroy?: () => void
}
customRenderers.interfaceNote: formScope.setField — change another form field (e.g. reset dependent). In repeater, formScope.repeater.updateItemField and live item reference are available. setError on result is called by the framework when error text changes — avoid redundant re-renders.
customRenderers.importantNote: Important: register renderers before first form open. With external libraries store instance locally in render(), not as class property; initialize synchronously — without setTimeout. custom-register
customRenderers.footerNote: Registry API: registerCustomFieldRenderer, registerCustomFieldRenderers, getCustomFieldRenderer, hasCustomFieldRenderer, unregisterCustomFieldRenderer, getAllCustomFieldRenderers — see /docs/core/methods. In repeater store DOM/widget instance locally in render(), remove listeners in destroy. Live examples — examples/vue3, examples/api-usage.

uploadUrlImportantNote.detailLabel: Learn more — image and file fields
uploadUrlImportantNote.text: Important: when using uploadUrl (upload via client API server), the server response must be an object with a src field containing the image URL. If the response format differs, use responseMapper to transform the response into an object with your fields and the required src field.
